cards.purpose: Governed desktop-control adapter for structured observation and gated terminal input on an operator-configured host.
prerequisites: Desktop-control binary present at /example/tools/gui-agent or configured by local override
Operating-system accessibility and input-control permissions granted on the current host
Desktop actions require audit refs and the formal execution gate for writes
cards.authority_boundary: Desktop execution helper only. It must not be treated as a governance bypass or a substitute for approval-gated write actions.
session_limitations: Only affects the current host desktop session
Focus drift or missing permissions should abort the action instead of guessing

host_overrides.note: Portable example hosts may lack the desktop-control binary; desktop work should fall back to shell or browser control when runtime facts confirm absence.
Example workstation hosts may carry full desktop-control permissions when configured by the operator.

cards.purpose: Persistent browser runtime for agents that can open pages, keep daemon-backed sessions alive across commands, and optionally attach to explicitly configured browser sessions.
prerequisites: browser-use CLI installed at /example/tools/browser-use or configured by local override
Wrapper-owned session or explicit browser-use session setup
cards.authority_boundary: Execution-only browser automation. Profile or CDP attach must be an explicit operator choice, not an assumed default.
session_limitations: Daemon-backed CLI sessions can persist across commands and reduce relaunch overhead
Login-dependent feeds often require explicit profile or CDP attach and may surface a real browser window

host_overrides.note: Portable example hosts can run browser-use locally, but personal browser session reuse remains explicit.

cards.purpose: Bounded AGN wrapper around browser-use that leaves screenshots, state dumps, kill-switch posture, and rollback evidence while preferring quiet background execution.
prerequisites: browser-use runtime binary available through local configuration
Wrapper kill switch off and emergency stop inactive
cards.authority_boundary: Controlled execution helper only. It does not mutate governance or bypass emergency stop.
session_limitations: Only wrapper-owned session IDs are valid
Produces evidence artifacts but does not become a general browser control authority

cards.purpose: Optional local worker lane for repetitive structured execution when a configured local model runtime is present.
prerequisites: Configured local model runtime is reachable
Configured local model path exists and is readable
Use remains bounded worker-grade, not final authority or flagship review
cards.authority_boundary: Worker-grade local model only. It must not be treated as a reviewer, governance judge, or destructive decision authority.
session_limitations: Host-local only; availability changes with storage mounts and runtime state
Unsuitable for final review or high-trust architectural judgment

host_overrides.note: Portable example hosts should assume qwen_local is absent unless runtime facts explicitly show external-storage-backed capacity.
Example workstation hosts may carry external-storage-backed local model capacity.

cards.purpose: Local Vertex proxy lane for Gemini-compatible execution when the operator-configured proxy endpoint is live.
prerequisites: Proxy process reachable on the current host
Task does not assume tools unsupported by the local proxy
cards.authority_boundary: Execution provider helper only. Configuration presence alone does not authorize or guarantee runtime use.
session_limitations: Host-local proxy availability can differ per machine and per session
Configured state is weaker than live endpoint reachability

host_overrides.note: Portable example hosts should not assume the local proxy is running even if credentials exist.
Workstation or Linux worker hosts may expose a reachable local proxy, but live endpoint checks outrank configuration.
